articles.title: Republicans push past the DHS shutdown, turning immigration fight into a budget test
articles.summary: Congressional Republicans are moving to end the long Department of Homeland Security shutdown with a party-line funding strategy that could lock in Trump’s immigration agenda for years.

articles.title: IMF Warns Iran War Has Shaken Global Growth and Reignited Inflation
articles.summary: The Fund cut its 2026 growth forecast to 3.1% and said higher energy costs are forcing policymakers back into a familiar trade-off: fighting inflation without choking off growth.

articles.title: JWST sharpens the case for airless rocky worlds around TRAPPIST-1
articles.summary: New James Webb measurements suggest two of the TRAPPIST-1 system’s most famous planets are probably bare rock, narrowing the search for potentially habitable atmospheres around nearby red dwarfs.

articles.title: CinemaCon’s biggest takeaway: Hollywood is betting the box office still belongs to theaters
articles.summary: At CinemaCon in Las Vegas, Paramount’s David Ellison promised 45-day exclusivity, Disney rolled out major franchise footage, and studios used the annual gathering to argue that moviegoing still matters.

articles.title: OpenAI pivots harder into enterprise AI as rival Anthropic presses its lead
articles.summary: OpenAI is re-centering its business around enterprise customers, agents, and infrastructure after a string of April announcements underscored how quickly the AI market has shifted from consumer novelty to workplace utility.

articles.title: Meta’s WhatsApp AI fight spotlights the new rules of the agent era
articles.summary: Europe’s pressure on Meta over WhatsApp access for rival chatbots is becoming a defining test of how regulators will police AI assistants, platform power and interoperability.

articles.title: Kenya’s ant-trafficking crackdown spotlights a niche hobby gone global
articles.summary: A case that started with live ants in specialized tubes has become a window into the booming, little-seen world of ant-keeping — and the conservation concerns it now raises.

articles.title: Israel-Lebanon talks test whether a fragile ceasefire can grow into diplomacy
articles.summary: After weeks of war and a U.S.-brokered truce, the first direct talks between Israel and Lebanon in decades are a narrow but potentially significant bid to turn battlefield pauses into a political track.

articles.title: BTS’s comeback tour is turning 2026 into a K-pop moment again
articles.summary: After nearly four years away, BTS is back on the road, back on Netflix and back at the center of pop culture’s global conversation.

articles.title: Lebanon ceasefire tests fragile regional diplomacy as U.S.-Iran talks hang in the balance
articles.summary: A 10-day truce in Lebanon has opened a narrow diplomatic window, but fighting, mistrust and the looming deadline for U.S.-Iran talks threaten to close it quickly.
